<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 数据模型定义
// ============================================

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Create observer relationship and test notification
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return std::pair<bool, int> Pair of whether observer was notified and call count
 */
std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Get the observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes with the graph
    graph.addNode(observer);
    graph.addNode(target);
    
    // Add observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    // Notify target of change
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // Return notification status and call count
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test thread manager registration and safety check
 *
 * @param enableSafety Whether to enable thread safety
 * @return bool Whether thread safety is enabled after operations
 */
bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    reaction::ThreadManager &tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Register current thread
    tm.registerThread();
    
    // 3. Enable thread safety if requested
    if (enableSafety) {
        tm.enableThreadSafety();
    }
    
    // 4. Return thread safety status
    return tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

   - `reaction::React::atomicAddAssign`: to perform an atomic addition.
   - `reaction::React::get`: to get the current value of the reactive variable.

 Steps:
  1. Include the necessary header (if not already included). However, note that the given code already includes <reaction/reaction.h>.
  2. Use `reaction::var` to create a reactive variable from `initialValue`.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition
 */
int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    // 创建响应式变量
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 执行原子加法操作
    reactiveVar.atomicAddAssign(addValue);
    
    // 获取最终值并返回
    return reactiveVar.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer node naming functionality
 *
 * @param name Name to set for the observer node
 * @return std::string The retrieved name from the observer graph
 */
std::string test_observer_naming(const std::string& name) {
    // 1. Create observer node
    auto observer_node = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Set node name
    graph.setName(observer_node, name);
    
    // 4. Return node name
    return graph.getName(observer_node);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return bool Whether batch operations work correctly
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto node1 = reaction::ObserverNode::create();
    auto node2 = reaction::ObserverNode::create();
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Create batch ID
    const void* batchId = graph.createBatchId();
    
    // 4. Register nodes to batch
    graph.addNodeToBatch(node1, batchId);
    graph.addNodeToBatch(node2, batchId);
    
    // 5. Verify nodes are in batch
    bool inBatch1 = node1->isInActiveBatch();
    bool inBatch2 = node2->isInActiveBatch();
    if (!inBatch1 || !inBatch2) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // 6. Unregister batch
    graph.unregisterActiveBatch(batchId);
    
    // 7. Verify nodes are no longer in batch
    bool notInBatch1 = !node1->isInActiveBatch();
    bool notInBatch2 = !node2->isInActiveBatch();
    if (!notInBatch1 || !notInBatch2) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // 8. Return success status
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer reset functionality
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return bool Whether observer reset worked correctly (observer no longer notified after reset)
 */
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observerNode = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto targetNode = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(targetName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship (target observes the observer)
    observerGraph.addOneObserver(targetNode, observerNode);
    
    // Notify target and verify observer was notified
    targetNode->valueChanged(true);
    if (!observerNode->wasChanged()) {
        return false; // Observer not notified initially
    }
    
    // Record call count before reset
    int initialCallCount = observerNode->getCallCount();
    
    // Reset observer node
    observerNode->reset();
    
    // Notify target again
    targetNode->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Verify observer was not notified after reset
    if (observerNode->wasChanged()) {
        return false; // Observer was notified after reset
    }
    
    // Ensure call count is reset to 0
    if (observerNode->getCallCount() != 0) {
        return false; // Call count not reset properly
    }
    
    return true; // Test passed
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test node closure functionality
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the observer node
 * @return bool Whether node closure worked correctly (relationships cleaned up)
 */
bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>("source");
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship (source observes target)
    graph.addOneObserver(source, observer);
    
    // Notify target and verify observer was notified
    source->notify(true);
    if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // Reset observer state
    observer->resetState();
    
    // Close observer node
    observer->close();
    
    // Notify target again
    source->notify(true);
    
    // Verify observer was not notified after closure
    if (observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }
    
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestTransactionalObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestTransactionalObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test transactional observer updates
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the node to update
 * @return bool Whether transactional update succeeded (no cycle created)
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>(nodeName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Set up initial relationship: source observes target
    graph.addObserver(source, target);
    
    // Notify target and verify relationship works
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Verify source was notified
    if (!source->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // Reset observer for next test
    source->reset();
    
    // Attempt transactional update that would create cycle
    bool transactionSuccess = false;
    try {
        // Transactional update: add self-observation (creates cycle)
        graph.updateObserversTransactional(source, {source});
        transactionSuccess = true; // Should not reach here due to cycle exception
    } catch (const reaction::CycleException&) {
        transactionSuccess = false;
    }
    
    // Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Verify original relationship is preserved
    if (!source->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }
    
    return !transactionSuccess; // Return true if cycle was detected and transaction failed
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestChainedObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestChainedObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test chained observer relationships
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observer1Name Name for the first observer node
 * @param observer2Name Name for the second observer node (observes observer1)
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether chain notification worked correctly
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Set up chain relationships
    graph.addObserver(target, observer1);
    graph.addObserver(observer1, observer2);
    
    // Notify target
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // Verify both observers were notified
    bool observer1Notified = observer1->getCallCount() == 1 && observer1->wasChanged();
    bool observer2Notified = observer2->getCallCount() == 1 && observer2->wasChanged();
    bool chainStatus = observer1Notified && observer2Notified;
    
    // Calculate total call count
    int totalCalls = target->getCallCount() + observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    
    return {totalCalls, chainStatus};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive action with side effects
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param newValue New value to set
 * @return int The value captured by the action (should be newValue after update)
 */
int test_reactive_action(int initialValue, int newValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variable to capture side effects
    int capturedValue = 0;
    
    // 3. Create reactive action
    auto reactiveAction = reaction::action([&]() {
        // Update reactive variable (this will trigger the action)
        reactiveVar.value(newValue);
        
        // Capture the value after update
        capturedValue = reactiveVar.get();
    });
    
    // 4. Update reactive variable (trigger the action)
    reactiveVar.value(newValue);
    
    // 5. Return captured value
    return capturedValue;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive update chain with multiple dependent calculations
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @param newA New value for a
 * @return int The result of the final calculation after updating a
 */
int test_reactive_update_chain(int a, int b, int newA) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto var1 = reaction::var(a);
    auto var2 = reaction::var(b);
    
    // Create first calculation: sum of var1 and var2
    auto calc1 = reaction::calc([](int x, int y) { return x + y; }, var1, var2);
    
    // Create second calculation: multiply calc1 by 2
    auto calc2 = reaction::calc([](int x) { return x * 2; }, calc1);
    
    // Update var1 to newA
    var1.value(newA);
    
    // Return the final result of calc2
    return calc2();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test resetting a calculation node with new dependencies
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @param c Third input value
 * @return int The result after resetting calculation to use different dependencies
 */
int test_calculation_reset(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto var_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    auto var_c = reaction::var(c);
    
    // Create initial calculation: a + b
    auto calc = reaction::calc([&]() { return var_a() + var_b(); });
    
    // Reset calculation with new dependencies: a + b + c
    calc.reset([&]() { return var_a() + var_b() + var_c(); });
    
    // Return the result after reset
    return calc();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.value(name); }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age.value(age); }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive fields updating calculated info
 *
 * @param initialName Initial person name
 * @param initialAge Initial person age
 * @param newName Updated person name
 * @param newAge Updated person age
 * @return std::string Concatenated info after updates
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    // Create reactive PersonRecord variable
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    
    // Create calculation depending on reactive fields
    auto info = reaction::calc([&person]() {
        return person.getName() + " is " + std::to_string(person.getAge()) + " years old.";
    });
    
    // Update name field
    person.setName(newName);
    
    // Update age field
    person.setAge(newAge);
    
    // Return updated info
    return info();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    
    // Create variable to track recomputation count
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    // Create calculation that depends on a and b
    auto sumCalc = reaction::calc([&recomputeCount, &a, &b]() {
        recomputeCount++;
        return a() + b();
    });
    
    // Perform non-batch updates
    a.value(nonBatchA);
    b.value(nonBatchB);
    
    // Record recompute count after non-batch updates
    int nonBatchCount = recomputeCount;
    
    // Create batch context and execute batch
    auto batch = reaction::batch([&a, &b]() {
        a.value(batchA);
        b.value(batchB);
    });
    batch.execute();
    
    // Record recompute count after batch updates
    int batchCount = recomputeCount;
    
    // Return final sum and counts
    return std::make_tuple(sumCalc(), nonBatchCount, batchCount);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test naming reactive nodes and retrieving names
 *
 * @param valueA Initial value for varA
 * @param valueB Initial value for varB
 * @return std::string Combined string of retrieved names
 */
std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    auto varA = reaction::var(valueA);
    varA.setName("varA");
    
    auto varB = reaction::var(valueB);
    varB.setName("varB");
    
    // Create calculations with names
    auto calcAB = reaction::calc([](int a, int b) { return a + b; }, varA, varB);
    calcAB.setName("calcAB");
    
    // Create action with name
    auto actAB = reaction::action([](int a, int b) { 
        return a * b; // Example action: multiply instead of add
    }, varA, varB);
    actAB.setName("actAB");
    
    // Retrieve names
    std::string nameVarA = varA.getName();
    std::string nameVarB = varB.getName();
    std::string nameCalc = calcAB.getName();
    std::string nameAction = actAB.getName();
    
    // Return combined names string
    return nameVarA + ", " + nameVarB + ", " + nameCalc + ", " + nameAction;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable (sensor)
    auto sensor = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Track recomputation count and last processed value
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    double lastProcessedValue = 0.0;
    
    // 3. Create filter-triggered calculation
    auto filteredCalc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recomputeCount++;
        double currentValue = sensor.get();
        lastProcessedValue = currentValue;
        
        // Determine status based on level threshold
        if (currentValue > levelThreshold) {
            return "HIGH";
        } else {
            return "NORMAL";
        }
    });
    
    // 4. Apply filter (only trigger when change exceeds diffThreshold)
    auto previousValue = reaction::var(initialValue);
    filteredCalc.filter([&]() {
        double current = sensor.get();
        double prev = previousValue.get();
        return std::abs(current - prev) >= diffThreshold;
    });
    
    // 5. Update sensor with first value
    sensor.value(firstUpdate);
    
    // 6. Record recompute count after first update
    int countAfterFirst = recomputeCount;
    
    // 7. Update sensor with second value
    sensor.value(secondUpdate);
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after second update
    int countAfterSecond = recomputeCount;
    
    // 9. Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = filteredCalc.get();
    
    // 10. Return results
    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, countAfterFirst, countAfterSecond);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Helper types
// ============================================

class FieldOwnerNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notificationCount++;
    }
    
    int getNotificationCount() const {
        return m_notificationCount;
    }

private:
    int m_notificationCount = 0;
};

class ProfileFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
        : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}
    
    reaction::Var<int>& score() { return m_score; }
    reaction::Var<bool>& active() { return m_active; }

private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_score;
    reaction::Var<bool> m_active;
};

struct FieldBindingResult {
    int notificationsAfterScoreUpdate;
    int notificationsAfterStatusUpdate;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    // Step 1: Create ProfileFields object
    ProfileFields profileFields(initialScore, true);
    
    // Step 2: Create FieldOwnerNode
    FieldOwnerNode ownerNode;
    
    // Step 3: Get observer graph instance and register owner node
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(&ownerNode);
    
    // Step 4: Get field graph instance and bind fields to owner node
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.bindFields(profileFields.getId(), &ownerNode);
    
    // Step 5: Update score field and record notification count
    profileFields.score() += scoreDelta;
    int scoreUpdateCount = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // Step 6: Update active field and record notification count
    profileFields.active() = false;
    int statusUpdateCount = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // Step 7: Return notification counts
    return FieldBindingResult{scoreUpdateCount, statusUpdateCount};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Helper types
// ============================================

class RecordingNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notificationCount() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct RollbackObservation {
    int notificationsAfterSecondBinding;
    int notificationsAfterRollback;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    // Create RecordingNode instances
    RecordingNode sensor1;
    RecordingNode sensor2;
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes (automatically done by ObserverNode constructor)
    
    // Set up initial dependency: sensor2 observes sensor1
    sensor2.observe(sensor1);
    
    // Save node state for rollback
    auto rollback = graph.saveNodeStateForRollback({&sensor1, &sensor2});
    
    // Replace dependencies transactionally: sensor2 now observes itself
    graph.resetNode(&sensor2);
    sensor2.observe(sensor2);
    
    // Trigger notification on second sensor
    sensor2.valueChanged();
    
    // Record notification count after second binding
    int after_second_binding = sensor2.notificationCount();
    
    // Execute rollback
    rollback();
    
    // Trigger notification on first sensor
    sensor1.valueChanged();
    
    // Record notification count after rollback
    int after_rollback = sensor2.notificationCount();
    
    return RollbackObservation{after_second_binding, after_rollback};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.reasoning:
<think>We are given a function signature and a struct for the result.
 We need to use the `reaction` library to create reactive variables, expressions, and calculations.
 Steps:
 1. Create a reactive variable (using `reaction::create`) with the initial value `baseValue`.
 2. Create a reactive expression that adds `increment` to the variable (using `reaction::create`).
 3. Create a reactive calculation that squares the variable (using `reaction::create`).
 4. Get the initial value of the expression (using `get` or `operator()`).
 5. Update the reactive variable by adding `increment` to it (using `value` method).
 6. Get the updated expression value.
 7. Get the squared value after the update.
 8. Return a `CreateFactoryResult` with these three values.

 Note: The reactive expressions and calculations are created from the variable, so they will update when the variable changes.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {
    int initialExpressionValue;
    int updatedExpressionValue;
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;
};

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::create(baseValue);
    
    // Create reactive expression: variable + increment
    auto reactiveExpression = reaction::create([reactiveVar]() {
        return reactiveVar() + increment;
    });
    
    // Create reactive calculation: square of the variable
    auto reactiveCalculation = reaction::create([reactiveVar]() {
        return reactiveVar() * reactiveVar();
    });
    
    // Get initial expression value
    int initialExpressionValue = reactiveExpression.get();
    
    // Update reactive variable
    reactiveVar.value(baseValue + increment);
    
    // Get updated expression value
    int updatedExpressionValue = reactiveExpression.get();
    
    // Get squared value after update
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate = reactiveCalculation.get();
    
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        initialExpressionValue,
        updatedExpressionValue,
        squaredValueAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>
#include <memory>
#include <unordered_set>

namespace test_reaction {

class NamedObserverNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    const std::string& name() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
};

struct ObserverCollectionSummary {
    std::size_t totalObservers;
    bool containsDirectObserver;
    bool containsTransitiveObserver;
};

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    // Create nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("source");
    auto direct = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("direct");
    auto intermediate = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("intermediate");
    auto transitive = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("transitive");

    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Register nodes
    graph.addNode(source);
    graph.addNode(direct);
    graph.addNode(intermediate);
    graph.addNode(transitive);

    // Set up direct observer relationship
    source->addOneObserver(direct);

    // Set up transitive observer relationship
    source->addOneObserver(intermediate);
    intermediate->addOneObserver(transitive);

    // Create NodeSet to store collected observers
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    graph.collectObservers(source, observers, 0);

    // Check if direct observer is in collected set
    bool has_direct = false;
    bool has_transitive = false;

    for (auto& obs : observers) {
        if (auto locked_obs = obs.lock()) {
            if (locked_obs->name() == direct->name()) {
                has_direct = true;
            }
            if (locked_obs->name() == transitive->name()) {
                has_transitive = true;
            }
        }
    }

    // Return collection summary
    return ObserverCollectionSummary{
        .totalObservers = observers.size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = has_direct,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = has_transitive
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

    - We can get the thread count with `getThreadCount()`

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyStatus {
    bool safetyBefore;
    bool safetyAfter;
    std::size_t threadCountBefore;
    std::size_t threadCountAfter;
};

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    tm.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    tm.registerThread();
    
    // 4. Get thread count and safety status before
    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountBefore = tm.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Create and start worker thread
    std::thread worker([&tm]() {
        // Worker thread registers itself
        tm.registerThread();
    });
    
    // 6. Wait for worker thread
    worker.join();
    
    // 7. Get thread count and safety status after
    bool safetyAfter = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountAfter = tm.getThreadCount();
    
    // 8. Return thread safety status
    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        safetyBefore,
        safetyAfter,
        threadCountBefore,
        threadCountAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    
    // Create variable to track recomputation count
    auto recomputeCount = reaction::var(0);
    
    // Create calculation that depends on reactiveVar and increments recomputeCount
    auto calculation = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recomputeCount += 1;
        return reactiveVar() + deltaA + deltaB;
    });
    
    // Record recompute count before batch
    int beforeBatchCount = recomputeCount();
    
    // Execute batch updates
    reaction::batchExecute([&]() {
        reactiveVar.value(baseValue + deltaA);
        // Update deltaA and deltaB in the batch (if needed)
        // Here we assume deltaA and deltaB are used as constants, so no need to update them
    });
    
    // Record recompute count after batch
    int afterBatchCount = recomputeCount();
    
    // Get final value
    int finalVal = calculation();
    
    return BatchExecuteResult{
        beforeBatchCount,
        afterBatchCount,
        finalVal
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {}
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    // Create CacheProbeNode instances
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeD = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes
    graph.registerNode(nodeA);
    graph.registerNode(nodeB);
    graph.registerNode(nodeC);
    graph.registerNode(nodeD);
    
    // Set up direct observer relationship
    nodeA->addOneObserver(nodeB);
    
    // Set up transitive observer relationship
    nodeB->addOneObserver(nodeC);
    nodeC->addOneObserver(nodeD);
    
    // Create NodeSet to store collected observers
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    
    // Populate cache data by collecting observers for nodeA
    graph.collectObservers(nodeA, observers);
    
    // Get cache statistics before cleanup
    auto statsBefore = graph.getCacheStats();
    
    // Trigger cache cleanup
    graph.triggerCacheCleanup();
    
    // Get cache statistics after cleanup
    auto statsAfter = graph.getCacheStats();
    
    // Calculate hit ratio after cleanup
    double hitRatio = 0.0;
    if (statsAfter.hitCount + statsAfter.missCount > 0) {
        hitRatio = static_cast<double>(statsAfter.hitCount) / 
                  (statsAfter.hitCount + statsAfter.missCount);
    }
    
    // Close all nodes
    nodeA->close();
    nodeB->close();
    nodeC->close();
    nodeD->close();
    
    // Return cache stats snapshot
    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        .graphEntriesBeforeCleanup = statsBefore.totalEntries,
        .graphEntriesAfterCleanup = statsAfter.totalEntries,
        .graphHitRatioAfterCleanup = hitRatio
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyTransition {
    bool beforeEnable;
    bool afterEnable;
    bool afterReset;
    std::size_t threadCount;
};

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    ThreadSafetyTransition transition;
    
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    threadManager.registerThread();
    
    // 4. Get thread count after registration
    transition.threadCount = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Get thread safety status before enabling
    transition.beforeEnable = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 6. Manually enable thread safety
    threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after enabling
    transition.afterEnable = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset thread manager state again
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 9. Get thread safety status after reset
    transition.afterReset = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return transition;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

  5. Update the mutable reactive variable (using `value` method on the reactive variable)
  6. Get the updated sum
  7. Return a `ConstVarResult` struct with the initial and updated sums

 Steps in code:
  - We'll create a mutable variable (say `mutableVar`) with the initial value `baseValue`.
  - We'll create a constant variable (say `constVar`) with the value `constValue`.
  - We'll create a calculation (say `sumCalc`) that returns the sum of `mutableVar` and `constVar`.
  - We'll get the initial sum by calling `sumCalc.get()`.
  - Then we update the mutable variable by calling `mutableVar.value(baseValue + delta)`.
  - Then we get the updated sum by calling `sumCalc.get()` again.
  - Return the `ConstVarResult` with the two sums.

 Note: The `reaction` library uses a reactive programming model where calculations are updated automatically when their dependencies change.

 However, note that the constant variable is not expected to change, so the calculation should only depend on the mutable variable after the update.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConstVarResult {
    int initialSum;
    int updatedSum;
};

ConstVarResult test_const_var_propagation(int baseValue, int constValue, int delta) {
    // Create a mutable reactive variable
    auto mutableVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    
    // Create a constant reactive variable
    auto constVar = reaction::constVar(constValue);
    
    // Create a calculation that sums both variables
    auto sumCalc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        return mutableVar.get() + constVar.get();
    });
    
    // Get the initial sum
    int initialSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    // Update the mutable reactive variable
    mutableVar.value(baseValue + delta);
    
    // Get the updated sum
    int updatedSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    return ConstVarResult{initialSum, updatedSum};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadGuardState {
    bool safetyBeforeGuard;
    bool safetyAfterGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard;
};

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Get thread safety status before guard
    bool safetyBeforeGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 4. Get thread count before guard
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Create a worker thread with ThreadRegistrationGuard
    std::thread worker([&threadManager]() {
        // ThreadRegistrationGuard will automatically register the thread
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard guard;
        // Worker thread does nothing else
    });
    
    // 6. Wait for worker thread to complete
    worker.join();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after guard
    bool safetyAfterGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Get thread count after guard
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 9. Return thread guard state
    return ThreadGuardState{
        safetyBeforeGuard,
        safetyAfterGuard,
        threadCountBeforeGuard,
        threadCountAfterGuard
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {
    int initialValue;
    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;
};

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Step 1: Create a reactive variable
    auto sourceVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Step 2: Create a calculation with LastHandle policy
    auto calcWithLastHandle = reaction::calc<reaction::LastHandle>([&]() {
        return sourceVar() * 2; // Example calculation: double the value
    });
    
    // Step 3: Get the initial result
    int initialResult = calcWithLastHandle();
    
    // Step 4: Update the source variable
    sourceVar.value(updatedValue);
    
    // Step 5: Get the updated result
    int updatedResult = calcWithLastHandle();
    
    // Step 6: Close the source node
    sourceVar.close();
    
    // Step 7: Get the result after source close
    int resultAfterClose = calcWithLastHandle();
    
    // Step 8: Return last handle snapshot
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initialResult,
        .afterUpdate = updatedResult,
        .afterSourceClose = resultAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 However, note that the `atomicOperation` method is defined in the `Resource` class and is templated. We can use it to perform the operation.

   - Call the `atomicOperation` method on the resource, passing a lambda that adds `delta` to the value, and a reference to our boolean.
   - The `atomicOperation` method will set the boolean to true if the value changed (i.e., if the operation actually modified the value).
   - Then, get the final value using the `get` method of the resource.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourceAtomicResult {
    int finalValue;
    bool operationReportedChange;
};

ResourceAtomicResult test_resource_atomic(int initialValue, int delta) {
    // Create a Resource instance with initial value
    reaction::Resource<int> resource(initialValue);
    
    // Variable to track if operation reported a change
    bool changeReported = false;
    
    // Perform atomic operation that adds delta to the value
    resource.atomicOperation([&delta](int& val) -> bool {
        val += delta;
        return true; // Change is always reported when we modify the value
    }, changeReported);
    
    // Get the final value
    int finalValue = resource.get();
    
    // Return the result
    return ResourceAtomicResult{finalValue, changeReported};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    std::size_t usageAfterGuard;
    double usagePercentage;
    bool nearLimitDuringGuard;
    bool monitoringAfterDisable;
};

    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::disableMonitoring();
    
    // Step 13: Check if monitoring is enabled after disable

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SmallPayload {
    int value;
    bool operator!=(const SmallPayload& other) const = default;
};

struct LargePayload {
    std::array<int, 64> data{};
    bool operator!=(const LargePayload& other) const = default;
};

struct SboStorageStatus {
    bool updateReportedChange;
    bool smallUsingSBO;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage;
    bool largeInitialized;
    bool largeUsingSBO;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                   int updatedSmall,
                                   int largeSeed) {
    // Create lambda to generate large payload with seed
    auto create_large_payload = [](int seed) -> LargePayload {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = seed + i;
        }
        return payload;
    };
    
    // Create small SBOResource and initialize with initial value
    reaction::memory::SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource;
    smallResource.updateValue(SmallPayload{initialSmall});
    
    // Create large SBOResource and initialize with generated payload
    reaction::memory::SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource;
    largeResource.updateValue(create_large_payload(largeSeed));
    
    // Update small resource and capture change report
    bool updateChanged = smallResource.updateValue(SmallPayload{updatedSmall});
    
    // Get storage information for both resources
    auto smallInfo = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    auto largeInfo = largeResource.getStorageInfo();
    
    // Populate status structure
    SboStorageStatus status;
    status.updateReportedChange = updateChanged;
    status.smallUsingSBO = smallInfo.using_sbo;
    status.smallStackUsage = smallInfo.stack_usage;
    status.largeInitialized = largeInfo.initialized;
    status.largeUsingSBO = largeInfo.using_sbo;
    
    return status;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 2. Reset thread manager state: Call `resetForTesting()` on the thread manager instance.
 3. Create ConditionalMutex instance: We'll create an instance of `reaction::ConditionalMutex`.
 4. Create ConditionalLockGuard in a scope block: We'll use `reaction::ConditionalLockGuard` to lock the mutex in a scope.
 5. Attempt to lock mutex without safety enabled: We note that without safety enabled, the lock might be a no-op.
 6. Exit guard scope: The guard will automatically unlock when it goes out of scope.
 7. Enable thread safety: Call `enableThreadSafety()` on the thread manager instance.
 8. Create ConditionalLockGuard in a scope block again: Lock the mutex again, but now with safety enabled.
 9. Attempt to lock mutex with safety enabled: Now the lock should behave as a real mutex.
 10. Exit guard scope: Again, the guard will unlock.

related functions.name: ConditionalSharedMutex
related functions.description: Shared mutex wrapper that only locks when thread safety is active

related functions.name: ConditionalSharedLockGuard
related functions.description: RAII shared lock guard honoring conditional locking behavior

related functions.name: resetForTesting
related functions.description: Reset ThreadManager internal state for deterministic mode

related functions.name: enableThreadSafety
related functions.description: Enable thread safety so conditional mutexes perform real locking

related functions.name: ConditionalSharedMutex::try_lock_shared
related functions.description: Attempt to acquire a shared lock (becomes no-op without safety)

related functions.name: ConditionalSharedMutex::try_lock
related functions.description: Attempt to acquire unique lock while respecting conditional behavior

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety;
    bool trySharedWithSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety;
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    reaction::ThreadManager &tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    tm.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Create ConditionalSharedMutex instance
    reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex mutex;
    
    // 4. Attempt to acquire shared lock without safety
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety = false;
    {
        trySharedWithoutSafety = mutex.try_lock_shared();
        
        // 5. Unlock shared lock if acquired
        if (trySharedWithoutSafety) {
            mutex.unlock_shared();
        }
    }
    
    // 6. Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard in a scope block
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = false;
    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLockGuard<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> guard(mutex);
        
        // 7. Attempt to acquire unique lock while shared lock is held without safety
        tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = mutex.try_lock();
    }
    
    // 10. Enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 11. Attempt to acquire shared lock with safety
    bool trySharedWithSafety = false;
    {
        trySharedWithSafety = mutex.try_lock_shared();
        
        // 12. Unlock shared lock if acquired
        if (trySharedWithSafety) {
            mutex.unlock_shared();
        }
    }
    
    // 13. Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard in a scope block again
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = false;
    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLockGuard<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> guard(mutex);
        
        // 14. Attempt to acquire unique lock while shared lock is held with safety
        tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = mutex.try_lock();
    }
    
    // 17. Return shared mutex result
    return SharedMutexResult{
        trySharedWithoutSafety,
        tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety,
        trySharedWithSafety,
        tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct TinyPod {
    int a;
    int b;
};

struct MediumPod {
    double values[3];
};

struct LargePod {
    std::byte payload[128];
};

struct SboTraitSummary {
    bool tinyAlwaysSbo;
    bool tinyForceHeap;
    bool mediumConditionalSbo;
    bool mediumForceHeap;
    bool largeForceHeap;
    std::size_t alwaysThreshold;
    std::size_t conditionalThreshold;
    std::size_t maxStackUsage;
};

SboTraitSummary analyze_sbo_traits() {
    SboTraitSummary summary;
    
    // Get SBO traits for TinyPod
    summary.tinyAlwaysSbo = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<TinyPod>::always_sbo;
    summary.tinyForceHeap = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<TinyPod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO traits for MediumPod
    summary.mediumConditionalSbo = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<MediumPod>::conditional_sbo;
    summary.mediumForceHeap = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<MediumPod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO traits for LargePod
    summary.largeForceHeap = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<LargePod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO configuration thresholds
    summary.alwaysThreshold = reaction::memory::SBOConfig::ALWAYS_SBO_SIZE;
    summary.conditionalThreshold = reaction::memory::SBOConfig::CONDITIONAL_SBO_SIZE;
    summary.maxStackUsage = reaction::memory::SBOConfig::MAX_SBO_STACK_USAGE;
    
    return summary;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResetBehaviorSnapshot {
    int beforeResetValue;
    int afterResetValue;
    bool safetyEnabledDuringReset;

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {

    threadManager.resetForTesting();

    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Step 4: Create a calculation
    auto calculation = reaction::calc([&reactiveVar, multiplier]() {
        return reactiveVar() * multiplier;
    });
    
    // Step 5: Get value before reset
    int beforeResetValue = calculation();
    
    // Step 6: Enable thread safety
    threadManager.enableThreadSafety();

    bool safetyEnabled = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    // Step 9: Update source variable
    reactiveVar.value(initialValue + 10);
    
    // Step 10: Get value after reset
    int afterResetValue = calculation();
    
    // Step 11: Return reset behavior snapshot
    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        beforeResetValue,
        afterResetValue,
        safetyEnabled
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.reasoning:
<think>We are given the function signature and the requirements. We need to implement the function `describe_error_codes` in the namespace `test_reaction`.
 The function should:
  1. Create a vector to store descriptions.
  2. Reserve space for optimization (the size is the number of input codes).
  3. Iterate through each error code in the input vector.
  4. Convert each code to a string using the `reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString` function.
  5. Return the vector of descriptions.

 Steps:
  - We are including the necessary headers: <reaction/core/exception.h> and <string> and <vector>.
  - We are in the namespace `test_reaction`.

 Implementation:

 We'll create a vector of strings, reserve space equal to the size of the input vector to avoid multiple allocations.
 Then, for each error code in the input vector, we convert it to a string using the static member function `reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString`.
 We push back each converted string into the result vector.

 Note: The function `errorCodeToString` is static, so we can call it by the class name.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::vector<std::string> describe_error_codes(
    const std::vector<reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode> &codes) {
    std::vector<std::string> descriptions;
    descriptions.reserve(codes.size());
    
    for (const auto& code : codes) {
        descriptions.push_back(reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString(code));
    }
    
    return descriptions;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<think>We are given the task to implement the `capture_exception_info` function using the `reaction` library.
 The function should create a ReactionException instance and then extract various pieces of information from it.

 Steps:
 1. Create a ReactionException instance with the given parameters.
 2. Use the ReactionException methods to get the formatted message, original message, file, line, function, and error code name.
 3. Populate the ExceptionInfo struct with these values and return it.

 However, note that the ReactionException class is defined in the `reaction` namespace and we are given the header file.

 We are also provided with the implementation of the ReactionException class, which includes the following methods:
   - getErrorCode() -> ErrorCode
   - getFile() -> const std::string&
   - getLine() -> int
   - getFunction() -> const std::string&
   - getOriginalMessage() -> const std::string&
   - errorCodeToString(ErrorCode) -> static std::string

 We can use these methods to get the required information.

 Important: The function `capture_exception_info` is in the namespace `test_reaction`.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ExceptionInfo {
    std::string formattedMessage;
    std::string originalMessage;
    std::string file;
    int line;
    std::string function;
    std::string codeName;
};

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,
    const std::string &file,
    int line,
    const std::string &function) {
    // Create ReactionException instance
    reaction::ReactionException exception(message, code, file, line, function);
    
    // Populate ExceptionInfo struct
    ExceptionInfo info;
    info.formattedMessage = exception.getFormattedMessage();
    info.originalMessage = exception.getOriginalMessage();
    info.file = exception.getFile();
    info.line = exception.getLine();
    info.function = exception.getFunction();
    info.codeName = reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString(code);
    
    return info;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct PointerAccessSnapshot {
    int valueAfterFirstAdd;
    int valueAfterSecondAdd;
    bool pointerStable;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate pointer-style access for reactive structs.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the Counter
 * @param delta Amount added on each pointer invocation
 * @return PointerAccessSnapshot Snapshot capturing results after pointer operations
 */
inline PointerAccessSnapshot test_react_pointer_access(int initialValue, int delta) {
    // Step 1: Define Counter struct
    struct Counter {
        int value;
        void add(int delta) { value += delta; }
    };
    
    // Step 2: Create reactive variable with Counter
    auto reactive_var = reaction::var(Counter{initialValue});
    
    // Step 3: Get first pointer
    auto first_ptr = reactive_var.getPtr();
    
    // Step 4: Call add method through pointer
    first_ptr->add(delta);
    
    // Step 5: Get current value after first add
    int value_after_first_add = reactive_var.get().value;
    
    // Step 6: Get second pointer
    auto second_ptr = reactive_var.getPtr();
    
    // Step 7: Call add method through pointer again
    second_ptr->add(delta);
    
    // Step 8: Get current value after second add
    int value_after_second_add = reactive_var.get().value;
    
    // Step 9: Check if pointer is stable
    bool pointer_stable = (first_ptr == second_ptr);
    
    // Step 10: Return pointer access snapshot
    return PointerAccessSnapshot{
        value_after_first_add,
        value_after_second_add,
        pointer_stable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto source = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Create copy of source
    auto copy = source;
    
    // Check validity before close
    bool firstValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    
    // Check equality before close
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);
    
    // Close source node
    source.close();
    
    // Check validity after close
    bool firstValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    
    // Check equality after close with exception handling
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    
    try {
        copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
    } catch (...) {
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    // Return validity snapshot
    return ValiditySnapshot{
        firstValidBeforeClose,
        firstValidAfterClose,
        copiesEqualBeforeClose,
        copiesEqualAfterClose,
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    // Create three reactive variables with names
    auto var1 = reaction::var(0).setName(firstName);
    auto var2 = reaction::var(0).setName(secondName);
    auto var3 = reaction::var(0).setName(thirdName);
    
    // Insert them into a set (which orders them by React::operator<)
    std::set<reaction::React> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);
    
    // Collect ordered names
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    orderedNames.reserve(3);
    for (const auto &react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.getName());
    }
    
    // Check if all names are unique (by comparing set size to number of elements)
    bool allUnique = reactSet.size() == 3;
    
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.reasoning:
<think>We are given the task to implement the function `test_graph_reset_node` using the `reaction` library.
 The function must perform a series of steps involving creating nodes, setting up an observer relationship, triggering notifications, resetting, and checking notifications.

 Steps:
 1. Create source and target ResetAwareNode instances.
 2. Get the observer graph instance.
 3. Register the nodes with the graph.
 4. Set up an observer relationship (source observes target).
 5. Trigger a notification on the source.
 6. Check if the target was notified (by checking its notification count) before reset.
 7. Clear the target's notification count.
 8. Reset the target node.
 9. Trigger a notification on the source again.
 10. Check if the target was notified after reset.
 11. Close the nodes (unregister them from the graph).
 12. Return a ResetNodeResult struct with the two boolean results.

 We are provided with the definition of `ResetAwareNode` and the `ResetNodeResult` struct.

 Implementation details:
 - We assume that the `reaction` library is included via the given includes.
 - We use the `reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance()` to get the graph.
 - We use `reaction::ObserverGraph::addNode` to register nodes.
 - We use `reaction::ObserverGraph::addObserver` to set up the observer relationship.
 - We trigger notifications by calling `valueChanged` on the source node (which is inherited from `ObserverNode`).
 - We check the notification count by calling `notifications` on the target node.
 - We reset the target node by calling `reset` on it (which is a member of `ResetAwareNode`).
 - We clear the notification count by calling `clear` on the target node.
 - We close the nodes by calling `close` on them (which is inherited from `ObserverNode`).

 Note: The `ResetAwareNode` class is defined in the `test_reaction` namespace and inherits from `reaction::ObserverNode`.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class ResetAwareNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit ResetAwareNode(std::string name) : m_name(name), m_notificationCount(0) {
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notificationCount;
    }
    
    void clear() {
        m_notificationCount = 0;
    }
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed) override {
        m_notificationCount++;
    }
    
private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notificationCount;
};

struct ResetNodeResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeReset;
    bool notifiedAfterReset;
};

ResetNodeResult test_graph_reset_node(const std::string &sourceName,
                                      const std::string &targetName) {
    // Create source and target nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(sourceName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(targetName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes
    graph.addNode(source);
    graph.addNode(target);
    
    // Set up observer relationship (source observes target)
    graph.addObserver(source, target);
    
    // Trigger notification on source
    source->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Check if target was notified before reset
    bool notifiedBefore = (target->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Clear target notification count
    target->clear();
    
    // Reset target node
    target->reset();
    
    // Trigger notification on source again
    source->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Check if target was notified after reset
    bool notifiedAfter = (target->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Close nodes
    source->close();
    target->close();
    
    return ResetNodeResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackAllocationSnapshot {
    bool smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithMonitoring;
    bool guardSucceeded;
    bool allocationBlockedAtLimit;
    bool runtimeAllowedAfterRelease;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate StackMonitor::canAllocate behaviour across modes.
 *
 * @param largeSize Value used for runtime canAllocate after release
 * @return StackAllocationSnapshot Snapshot of allocation checks before/after guard
 */
inline StackAllocationSnapshot test_stack_monitor_capacity(std::size_t largeSize) {
    // 1. Disable monitoring
    reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().setMonitoringEnabled(false);
    
    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().initialize();
    
    // 3. Check allocation without monitoring
    bool smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(16);
    bool largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);
    bool runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(32);
    
    // 4. Enable monitoring
    reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().setMonitoringEnabled(true);
    
    // 5. Initialize stack monitor again
    reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().initialize();
    
    // 6. Check large allocation with monitoring
    bool largeAllowedWithMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);
    
    // 7. Create StackGuard in scope block
    bool guardSucceeded = false;
    {
        constexpr size_t guardSize = 64 * 1024; // Fixed compile-time size for StackGuard
        bool guardAllowed = reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(guardSize);
        guardSucceeded = guardAllowed;
        reaction::StackGuard<guardSize> guard; // StackGuard uses RAII
        
        // 8. Check if guard succeeded (via internal allocation)
        // 9. Check if allocation blocked at limit (fixed large size beyond limit)
        bool allocationBlocked = !reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(64 * 1024 + 1);
        
        // Store results for later return
        guardSucceeded = guardAllowed;
        allocationBlockedAtLimit = allocationBlocked;
    }
    
    // 11. Check runtime allocation after release
    bool runtimeAllowedAfterRelease = reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);
    
    return StackAllocationSnapshot{
        smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring,
        largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring,
        runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring,
        largeAllowedWithMonitoring,
        guardSucceeded,
        allocationBlockedAtLimit,
        runtimeAllowedAfterRelease
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // Step 1: Create owner and field TrackingFieldNode instances
    auto owner = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("owner");
    auto field = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("field");
    
    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance and register nodes
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(owner);
    observerGraph.registerNode(field);
    
    // Step 3: Get field graph instance and register field nodes under object ID
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.registerField(objectId, field);
    
    // Step 4: Bind field to owner
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, field, owner);
    
    // Step 5: Trigger notification on field
    field->notify();
    
    // Step 6: Check if owner was notified before delete
    bool notifiedBefore = (owner->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Step 7: Clear owner notification count
    owner->clear();
    
    // Step 8: Clear dependencies on owner
    observerGraph.clearNodeDependents(owner);
    
    // Step 9: Remove field mappings for object ID
    fieldGraph.removeObjFieldMap(objectId);
    
    // Step 10: Attempt to bind field again
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, field, owner);
    
    // Step 11: Trigger notification on field again
    field->notify();
    
    // Step 12: Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfter = (owner->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Step 13: Close nodes
    owner->close();
    field->close();
    
    // Step 14: Return field graph cleanup result
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 2. Check if the resource uses SBO storage (using isUsingSBO() method).

 7. Assign a node name (using ObserverGraph::getInstance().setName(resource.getPtr(), nodeName)).
 8. Check if the name was assigned (using ObserverGraph::getInstance().getName(resource.getPtr()) == nodeName).
 9. Close the node (using ObserverGraph::getInstance().closeNode(resource.getPtr())).

11. Return a VoidResourceSnapshot with the results.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <type_traits>

namespace test_reaction {

struct VoidResourceSnapshot {
    bool retrievedIsVoid;
    bool usesSboStorage;
    bool nameAssigned;
    bool nameClearedAfterClose;
};

inline VoidResourceSnapshot test_resource_void_behavior(const std::string &nodeName) {
    // 1. Create shared Resource<Void> instance
    auto resource = std::make_shared<reaction::Resource<Void>>();
    
    // 2. Check if resource uses SBO storage
    bool uses_sbo = resource->isUsingSBO();
    
    // 3. Get value from resource
    auto value = resource->getValue();
    
    // 4. Check if retrieved value is Void type
    bool is_void = std::is_same_v<decltype(value), reaction::Void>;
    
    // 5. Get observer graph instance
    auto &observer_graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 6. Register resource node
    observer_graph.addNode(resource);
    
    // 7. Assign node name
    observer_graph.setName(resource, nodeName);
    
    // 8. Check if name was assigned
    bool name_assigned = (observer_graph.getName(resource) == nodeName);
    
    // 9. Close node
    observer_graph.closeNode(resource);
    
    // 10. Check if name was cleared after close
    bool name_cleared = (observer_graph.getName(resource) == "");
    
    // 11. Return void resource snapshot
    return VoidResourceSnapshot{
        .retrievedIsVoid = is_void,
        .usesSboStorage = uses_sbo,
        .nameAssigned = name_assigned,
        .nameClearedAfterClose = name_cleared
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    // Step 1: Create CountingObserver instance
    auto counting_observer = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    
    // Step 2: Create three source ObserverNode instances
    auto source1 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source2 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source3 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    
    // Step 3: Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 4: Register all nodes
    graph.addNode(counting_observer);
    graph.addNode(source1);
    graph.addNode(source2);
    graph.addNode(source3);
    
    // Step 5: Attach single observer (CountingObserver) to first source
    source1->updateObservers(counting_observer);
    
    // Step 6: Trigger notification on first source
    source1->notify();
    
    // Step 7: Get notification count after initial setup
    int first_initial_count = counting_observer->notifications();
    
    // Step 8: Rewire dependencies - detach from source1 and attach to source2
    counting_observer->updateObservers(); // Clear current observers
    counting_observer->updateObservers(source2);
    
    // Step 9: Trigger notification on old source (source1)
    source1->notify();
    int first_after_update_count = counting_observer->notifications();
    
    // Step 10: Trigger notification on second source
    source2->notify();
    int second_after_update_count = counting_observer->notifications();
    
    // Step 11: Trigger notification on third source
    source3->notify();
    int third_after_update_count = counting_observer->notifications();
    
    // Step 12: Close all nodes
    counting_observer->close();
    source1->close();
    source2->close();
    source3->close();
    
    // Step 13: Return observer node link snapshot
    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        .firstNotificationsAfterInitial = first_initial_count,
        .firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = first_after_update_count,
        .secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = second_after_update_count,
        .thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = third_after_update_count
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
